nodes.x: NOW it came to pass in the thirtieth year of the reign of the judges over the people of Nephi, after Moroni had received and had read Helaman's epistle, he was exceedingly rejoiced because of the…

1-4 Moroni was exceedingly rejoiced because of the exceeding success which Helaman had had. Moroni received the epistle from Helaman in the early part of the Thirtieth year of the Judges, and upon him reading it he was filled with joy and thanksgiving for he knew that no man without the help of the Lord could accomplish the mighty things that Helaman had brought to an issue of full success. Nor…
Yea, and he did make it known unto all his people, in all the land round about in that part where he was, that they might rejoice also.
And it came to pass that he immediately sent an epistle to Pahoran, desiring that he should cause men to be gathered together to strengthen Helaman, or the armies of Helaman, insomuch that he might…
And it came to pass when Moroni had sent this epistle to the land of Zarahemla, he began
And it came to pass that while Moroni was thus making preparations to go against the Lamanites to battle, behold, the people of Nephihah, who were gathered together from the city of Moroni and the…

5-8 The people of Nephihah were attacked by the Lamanites. Inspired and inspirited by Helaman's success in regaining from the Lamanites the lands and cities of the Nephites situated in the southwestern sector of greater Zarahemla, Moroni, after sending his epistle to Pahoran, began to map out the strategy in which he saw the fulfillment of his desires for the conquered cities along the Atlantic…
Yea, even those who had been compelled to flee from the land of Manti, and from the land round about, had come over and joined the Lamanites in this part of the land.
And thus being exceedingly numerous, yea, and receiving strength from day to day, by the command of Ammoron they came forth against the people of Nephihah, and they did begin to slay them with an…
And their armies were so numerous that the remainder of the people of Nephihah were obliged to flee before them; and they came even and joined the army of Moroni.
And now as Moroni had supposed that there should be men sent to the city of Nephihah, to the assistance of the people to maintain that city, and knowing that it was easier to keep the city from…
Therefore he retained all his force to maintain those places which he had recovered.
And now, when Moroni saw that the city of Nephihah was lost he was exceedingly sorrowful, and began to doubt, because of the wickedness of the people, whether they should not fall into the hands of…

9-13 When Moroni saw that the City of Nephihah was lost he was exceeding sorrowful. Amid all his cares and preparations for the struggle before him, Moroni was forced to rely on those in governmental positions to lessen his task by fulfilling their allotted requirements. He had been assured, or at least led to think, that troops to the proper number would be sent to bolster the defenses of…
Now this was the case with all his chief captains. They doubted and marveled also because of the wickedness of the people, and this because of the success of the Lamanites over them.
And it came to pass that Moroni was angry with the government, because of their indifference concerning the freedom of their country.
